notes: This plan was authored by Claude, outside opencode, based on direct inspection of the current repo state (not assumptions). Two things in here need YOUR decision before a coding agent should touch them — see 'open_decisions' below.

Agent names below use 'coder' and 'test-writer' to match the roles currently defined in opencode.json. The prior tasks.json in this repo used 'build'/'test' instead — that naming mismatch is unresolved; confirm which is correct for your current opencode config before running this.

Any BLS series ID, Census ACS variable code, or crime API endpoint marked '[VERIFY]' below is not confirmed against current API docs. Do not let a coding agent guess at these — have it (or you) check the live docs first.

open_decisions.question: GrowthArea (city-level, has composite_score) and MarketSnapshot (ZIP-level, populated by refresh_market_data) are disconnected. The HTML /growth/ page reads MarketSnapshot; the API growth_areas_list endpoint reads GrowthArea. Populating GrowthArea (this plan) will NOT change what /growth/ shows.
options: A) Keep both models, both purposes distinct (API = metro-level growth ranking, HTML = ZIP-level snapshot browsing). No merge needed, just document the split clearly.
B) Make /growth/ HTML page also read GrowthArea once populated (small view change).
C) Deprecate one model in favor of the other long-term (bigger refactor, not in this plan).
open_decisions.default_assumption_in_this_plan: None taken — TASK-B5 is a no-op placeholder until you pick an option.

open_decisions.question: crime.py has no real adapter at all (fully hardcoded dummy). Unlike rents/schools/walkscore, there's no already-built fetch_* function to wire up — this needs a new integration picked from scratch.
options: A) FBI Crime Data Explorer API — I believe this may not require a key for basic access via api.usa.gov, but I am not certain of current auth requirements or endpoint stability. [VERIFY before committing to this]
B) A paid crime-index API (e.g. one requiring its own key/budget like ATTOM) — more reliable but adds another API key + cost line to track.
C) Skip real crime data for MVP; leave dummy but relabel it clearly in the UI as 'placeholder' rather than presenting it as real.
open_decisions.default_assumption_in_this_plan: TASK-A1 is written as a spike/investigation task first, not a direct implementation, because I can't confirm option A's key requirements.

tasks.title: SPIKE: Confirm a real crime-data source and its auth requirements
acceptance_criteria: Written finding (comment or short doc) confirming: exact API base URL, whether an API key is required, rate limits, and geography level supported (ZIP vs county vs city)
No code written in this task — investigation only
If no free/reliable source is found, explicitly recommend DECISION-2 option C (relabel dummy as placeholder) instead of building against an unverified API

tasks.title: Implement real crime.py adapter based on TASK-A1 findings
acceptance_criteria: New fetch_crime_score(zip_code, api_key=None) added, following the existing pattern in rents.py/schools.py (cache check, graceful None on missing key/HTTP failure)
Existing get_crime_score() dummy function left untouched and unchanged for backward compatibility
Result cached with a TTL consistent with data source's actual update frequency (do not reuse 30-day TTL from schools.py without checking whether crime data source updates more/less often)

tasks.title: Implement real comps.py adapter using existing ATTOMAdapter
acceptance_criteria: New fetch_comps_for_listing(listing, api_key=None) added, calling ATTOMAdapter().fetch_sales_history(address) and/or fetch_avm_detail(address) — reuses ATTOM_API_KEY, no new key required
Returns list of dicts matching the existing dummy's shape: {address, price, sq_ft, ppsf} so callers don't need to change
Existing get_comps_for_listing() dummy function left untouched
Handles ATTOMAPIError/ATTOMRateLimitError by returning empty list, not raising, consistent with market_data.py's try/except wrapping pattern

tasks.title: Wire refresh_market_snapshot() to real rent + school adapters
acceptance_criteria: core/services/market_data.py calls fetch_rent_estimate(address, api_key, zip_code) when RENTCAST_API_KEY is set in environment; falls back to existing dummy get_rent_estimate_for_listing() when key is absent
Same pattern for fetch_school_rating(zip_code, api_key) / GREATSCHOOLS_API_KEY / get_school_rating()
Fallback-to-dummy path is logged at WARNING level so it's visible in logs when real data isn't being used
No behavior change when API keys are absent (safe to merge before you've registered for RentCast/GreatSchools)

tasks.title: Wire refresh_market_snapshot() to real crime + comps adapters
acceptance_criteria: Same key-present/fallback-to-dummy pattern as TASK-A4, applied to crime and comps

MarketSnapshot rows created via this path are distinguishable from dummy-sourced ones (e.g. a data_source or is_placeholder-style field, or at minimum a log line) — exact mechanism up to implementer, but silence is not acceptable given this was flagged as a credibility issue

tasks.title: Tests for TASK-A2 through TASK-A5
acceptance_criteria: Mirrors the existing test pattern for rents/schools/walkscore (see core/tests/ — exact filenames not confirmed by me, locate via `pytest --collect-only`)
Covers: missing key -> dummy fallback with WARNING log; HTTP failure -> dummy fallback; successful real response -> real value persisted
No live API calls in tests — mock requests/ATTOMAdapter calls

tasks.title: Update core/integrations/README.md to document market/ adapters
acceptance_criteria: README currently documents only ATTOM + HUD + health monitor — add a section covering core/integrations/market/ (census, bls, rents, schools, walkscore, crime, comps) with real vs dummy status for each, post-this-plan
Explicitly state which adapters require which env var keys

tasks.title: Extend census.py to compute real population + income growth (two-vintage comparison)
acceptance_criteria: New function (e.g. fetch_place_growth_metrics) calls the existing ACS endpoint twice at two vintages (e.g. current ACS_VINTAGE and one 5 years prior) for the same geography, using variables B01001_001E (population) and B19013_001E (income) — same pattern already used in fetch_zip_demographics, just called twice and diffed

Uses Census geography level 'place' (city) or 'metropolitan statistical area', not 'zip code tabulation area' — GrowthArea is city/metro-level, not ZIP-level, so the existing ZCTA geography param must be changed, not reused as-is
Reuses existing CENSUS_API_KEY — no new key

_estimate_population_growth's current always-returns-None behavior is superseded by this real calculation for the GrowthArea path (ZIP-level fetch_zip_demographics can be left as-is for MarketSnapshot)

tasks.title: SPIKE + implement: BLS employment growth (not just unemployment rate)
acceptance_criteria: [VERIFY] Confirm the correct BLS LAUS series ID suffix for employment level (not the 0000000003 unemployment-rate suffix already used in bls.py) against BLS's series ID documentation before writing code — I do not have this confirmed
New function fetches employment level at two time points for the same geography and computes % growth, mirroring the two-call pattern in TASK-B1
Reuses existing BLS_API_KEY — no new key

If county/metro-level BLS series require a different ID scheme than the state-level LAUS series already used (e.g. QCEW), document that difference in a code comment rather than silently mixing schemes

tasks.title: Housing demand proxy from Census ACS housing occupancy data
acceptance_criteria: This is an explicitly proposed heuristic, not an official 'housing demand index' — label it as such in code comments and any UI

[VERIFY] variable codes before use: ACS table B25002 (Occupancy Status) — B25002_001E total units, B25002_003E vacant units — confirm against the Census variables list for whichever ACS vintage is used, e.g. https://api.census.gov/data/{vintage}/acs/acs5/variables.html

Proposed formula: lower vacancy rate + higher population growth (from TASK-B1) => higher demand index; exact weighting is a product decision, not something I'm asserting is correct — implementer/you should sanity-check output against a few known-tight vs known-soft markets before trusting it
Reuses existing CENSUS_API_KEY

tasks.title: Management command: populate_growth_areas
acceptance_criteria: Follows the existing refresh_market_data.py command pattern: argparse for --state and --city (or a list file), cache-freshness check, update_or_create upsert, styled stdout output
Calls TASK-B1/B2/B3 functions and upserts GrowthArea rows (state, city_name, metro_area, population_growth_rate, employment_growth_rate, median_income_growth, housing_demand_index, data_timestamp)
housing_demand_index on the model is an IntegerField — output from TASK-B3 must be scaled/rounded to an int, document the scale chosen (e.g. 0-100) since none is defined in the current model
No new API keys required — command should fail clearly with a CommandError if CENSUS_API_KEY or BLS_API_KEY is missing, same style as refresh_market_data.py's warnings

tasks.title: Resolve or document the GrowthArea/MarketSnapshot split (blocked on DECISION-1)
acceptance_criteria: Do not start this task until DECISION-1 above is answered by the human
If option B chosen: update core/views.py growth_areas() to read from GrowthArea instead of/in addition to MarketSnapshot
If option A chosen: this task becomes documentation-only — add a comment/doc explaining the intentional split so future contributors don't 'fix' it by accident

tasks.title: Tests for TASK-B1 through TASK-B4
acceptance_criteria: Mocks all Census/BLS HTTP calls — no live API calls in tests
Covers: successful two-vintage growth calculation, missing data in one vintage (partial failure -> None or 0, decide and document which), missing API key -> command fails clearly
Covers management command upsert behavior (create vs update) mirroring existing test_market_adapters.py conventions

new_api_keys_required.phase_a_dummy_adapters: RENTCAST_API_KEY and GREATSCHOOLS_API_KEY (free, placeholders already in .env.example — you need to actually register and fill values in). Crime source depends on TASK-A1 outcome — possibly none, possibly one, unconfirmed. Comps needs no new key (reuses ATTOM_API_KEY).
